content.text: mira's comfort object for naps (and travel) is her **blue elephant** — that's what you've noted works for her. keeping it consistently in the same spot as part of the nap wind-down helps her brain connect the object with "sleep is coming," which is especially useful at 18 months when toddlers are starting to assert more independence around rest. if you ever need a backup for travel or washing days, grabbing an identical second one now (while it's still available) is a practical next step many parents find saves a lot of stress down the road.
